## What was planned

**Story #9 â€” Paginate the receivables report, standardize default page size to 25, and index the query**
https://github.com/dsj1984/bench-sbx-2026-07-23-brownfield-longitudinal-mandrel-47a00acd/issues/9

One cohesive Story (default-single policy â€” the three asks are tightly coupled around the same pagination/reporting surface). It captures all three requirements:

1. **Paginate `GET /reports/receivables`** â€” `page`/`pageSize` query params and `items`/`total`/`page`/`pageSize` envelope mirroring the sibling list endpoints, with `totalOutstandingCents` preserved as the grand total across *all* pages (a full-population aggregate, not a sum of the returned slice).
2. **Default page size 20 â†’ 25** â€” routed through one shared constant so every paginated endpoint moves together and the default lives in exactly one place.
3. **Sub-2s at scale** â€” a supporting DB index plus a seeded (â‰Ą5000 orders) performance test asserting <2000ms.

Acceptance criteria are outcome-focused and each backed by a `verify` command (three contract tests + `npm run typecheck`).

## Notes for the record

- **Baseline is empty.** This cell's tree holds only the sandbox template (0 source files), so the plan-context codebase snapshot was empty. The change request presupposes existing endpoints, so I declared the modified handlers as `refactors-existing`; the validator deterministically auto-normalized them to `creates` against the untracked base (3 warnings, non-blocking) â€” the honest brownfield authoring behaving as designed. The Story's `## Spec` records the endpoint-convention assumptions the delivery agent must verify against the live tree.
- **Gates:** both plan critics returned `dispatch: false`, text-hygiene clean; the dry-run pre-pass exercised every persist gate write-free before the real persist.
- Per instructions, I ran **only** the planning pipeline â€” no delivery. Deliver with `/deliver 9` when ready.

Delivered. The terminal envelope reports **`status: landed`** â€” PR #10 squash-merged into `main`, Story #9 flipped to `agent::done`, all close gates green.

## Summary

**Story #9** â€” *Paginate the receivables report, standardize default page size to 25, and index the query* â€” is delivered and merged.

The Story was framed as "refactors-existing," but verifying against the live tree (as its Key Assumptions instruct) showed the API didn't exist yet â€” the sandbox baseline had only `package.json`/`README`. So delivering the capability meant building the coherent slice the plan assumed, on `node:http` + built-in `node:sqlite` (no new deps):

- **`src/lib/pagination.js`** â€” the single source of truth. `DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE = 25` (was 20), plus shared `parsePagination`/`offsetFor`/`buildPageEnvelope`. Every paginated endpoint reads it.
- **`src/reports/receivables.js`** + **`src/routes/reports.js`** â€” `GET /reports/receivables` now returns the standard `items`/`total`/`page`/`pageSize` envelope, with `totalOutstandingCents` kept as the full-population grand total across all pages (computed independently of the page slice).
- **`src/routes/orders.js`** â€” a sibling `/orders` list endpoint sharing the same contract, so "consistent default across every endpoint" is real and testable.
- **`db/migrations/add-orders-receivables-index.sql`** â€” a partial index `idx_orders_receivables (created_at DESC, id DESC) WHERE outstanding_cents > 0` covering the report's filter + ordering.

**Acceptance (all AC verify commands exit 0):**
- Pagination envelope + grand-total independence â€” `reports-receivables.pagination.test.js` âś”
- Default page size 25, single shared constant, no endpoint still on 20 â€” `pagination-default.test.js` âś”
- 6000 orders â†’ responds in ~50ms (< 2000ms), query plan confirmed using the index â€” `reports-receivables.performance.test.js` âś”
- `npm run typecheck` clean (11 files); full suite 10/10.

**Delivery ceremony:** close-validation gates passed; Story-scope code review (clean-code / data-model / performance lenses) â†’ 0 critical/high/medium, 1 suggestion; PR merged via native auto-merge.

**PR:** https://github.com/dsj1984/bench-sbx-2026-07-23-brownfield-longitudinal-mandrel-47a00acd/pull/10

One non-fatal note from the tail: `baseFastForward` was skipped because the main checkout's working tree is dirty (untracked benchmark `temp/` and harness files) â€” cosmetic local hygiene only; the merge stands. Single-Story run, so the per-run epilogue is correctly skipped.
